skill.name: nowait-reasoning-optimizer
skill.description: Adds NOWAIT-style token suppression for reasoning models to reduce unnecessary self-reflection tokens during local inference.
skill.summary: Reduce verbose reasoning tokens with configurable NOWAIT logit suppression.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many high-risk patterns, but review shows the backtick findings are Markdown code spans or fenced examples, not shell execution. The certificate, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance detections are keyword false positives in documentation and token-iteration code. The remaining risk is low because the skill includes a local Python helper that modifies model generation behavior but shows no network, subprocess, environment access, or file-write behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static external-command detections in SKILL.md and refrences/keywords.md occur in Markdown code fences, inline code spans, and Python examples. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are rendered documentation or fenced Python snippets. They are not executable shell contexts and contain no subprocess invocation.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File And Weak Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The detected terms are words such as keywords, key decision points, token IDs, and NOWAIT text. No evidence found of certificate files, private keys, hashing, encryption, or weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the lines describe reasoning keywords and token matching. There is no cryptographic API use or sensitive file material.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The flagged locations iterate tokenizer vocabulary, scores, and local token IDs for logit suppression. No evidence found of host reconnaissance, system inventory collection, or environment probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is model-tokenizer processing, not operating-system discovery. The code does not read hostnames, users, processes, files, or network state.

low_findings.title: Local Inference Helper Requires User Review
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE LOW RISK: scripts/nowait_processor.py contains executable Python that changes logits and can call a caller-provided model.generate method. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users should review model and tokenizer sources before running local inference code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The helper directly invokes local generation with caller-provided objects. The behavior is legitimate, with no evidence of exfiltration or command execution.

content.value_statement: Reasoning models can spend many tokens on repeated self-reflection. This skill provides NOWAIT guidance and a local processor to reduce those tokens during inference.
actual_capabilities: Explains when NOWAIT token suppression is useful for R1-style reasoning models.
Provides a Python logit processor for suppressing reflection keyword tokens.
Builds bad_words_ids lists for frameworks that support blocked token IDs.
Includes default reflection keywords and excluded false-positive patterns.
Documents model families where token reduction is expected or risky.
Offers a stopping-criteria alternative to full logit suppression.
limitations: It does not train, fine-tune, or evaluate a model automatically.
Accuracy can degrade on distilled models and difficult reasoning tasks.
Keyword lists may need adjustment for each tokenizer and domain.
The included script depends on local PyTorch and model runtime setup.

use_cases.title: Reduce Production Inference Cost
use_cases.description: Apply token suppression to verbose reasoning models before measuring latency, cost, and answer quality.

use_cases.title: Test Reasoning Efficiency
use_cases.description: Compare baseline reasoning outputs with NOWAIT outputs across math, multimodal, or verification-heavy tasks.

use_cases.title: Integrate Blocked Token Lists
use_cases.description: Generate bad_words_ids for serving frameworks that support token blocking during model generation.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Fit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check before using the skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to decide whether NOWAIT is appropriate for my model and task. My model is [model name], and my task is [task].

prompt_templates.title: Create Integration Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Add NOWAIT to an existing inference flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline how to add NOWAIT to my Transformers generation pipeline. Include setup steps, validation checks, and rollback criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Keywords
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my reasoning outputs and suggest a conservative NOWAIT keyword list for [domain]. Preserve essential verification language.

prompt_templates.title: Design Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an A/B evaluation for NOWAIT. Include token savings, latency, accuracy, failure modes, and model-specific risk checks.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether NOWAIT is safe for a Qwen3 distilled model on hard math problems.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend caution. It would explain that distilled Qwen3 variants can lose accuracy when reflection tokens are suppressed.

output_examples.input: Plan a rollout for NOWAIT in a local Transformers inference service.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce a staged plan covering tokenizer review, keyword tuning, baseline comparison, latency measurement, and rollback thresholds.

output_examples.input: Explain why a token was suppressed during generation.
output_examples.output: The skill would trace the token to a configured reflection keyword and note whether an excluded pattern should prevent suppression.

best_practices: Benchmark accuracy and token savings on representative tasks before deployment.
Start with conservative keyword suppression, then expand only after reviewing failures.
Keep separate settings for RL-based, distilled, and multimodal model families.
anti_patterns: Do not suppress reflection tokens on safety-critical tasks without human review.
Do not assume the default keyword list fits every tokenizer or language.
Do not compare token savings without also measuring answer quality.

faq.question: What does this skill optimize?
faq.answer: It targets repeated self-reflection tokens in reasoning model outputs to reduce generation length.

faq.question: Does it change model weights?
faq.answer: No. It is an inference-time technique that adjusts token logits during generation.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported marketplace tools.

faq.question: Can it hurt accuracy?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation warns that distilled models can lose accuracy on challenging tasks.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network access in the reviewed files. Local model setup may have separate requirements.

faq.question: What should users verify first?
faq.answer: Users should verify tokenizer behavior, model family, benchmark accuracy, and the final keyword list.
